Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. 2026-04-09T08:43:53.9345889Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T08:34:48.083492937Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57987,\"title\":\"[BillingBenefits] Docs: Update README and CHANGELOG after TypeSpec migration adding new resource types\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57779 by `@haiyuazhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nThe billingbenefits management SDK was migrated from Swagger/AutoRest to TypeSpec (MPG migration). This migration added **8 new resource types** to the package that were not present before:\\n\\n- `MaccResource` / `MaccData` / `MaccCollection` (Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment)\\n- `CreditResource` / `CreditData` / `CreditCollection`\\n- `CreditSourceResource` / `CreditSourceData` / `CreditSourceCollection`\\n- `FreeServicesResource` / `FreeServicesData` / `FreeServicesCollection`\\n- `ConditionalCreditResource` / `ConditionalCreditData`\\n- `ConditionalCreditContributorResource` / `ConditionalCreditContributorData`\\n- `ContributorResource`\\n- `DiscountResource` / `DiscountCollection`\\n\\nThe pre-existing resources (`BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource`, `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanResource`, `BillingBenefitsReservationOrderAliasResource`, `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderAliasResource`) remain.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG empty for v1.0.0-beta.5**: The unreleased changelog entry has no content despite the significant TypeSpec migration adding 8+ new resource types\\n- **README Key Concepts section is generic**: Only links to the Azure SDK intro page; does not describe any of the actual BillingBenefits resources (SavingsPlans, Reservations, MACC, Credits, FreeServices, ConditionalCredits, Discounts, Contributors) or what each is used for\\n- **README Examples section lacks code snippets**: Only links to a generic samples page; no inline code examples demonstrating common operations like listing savings plans, getting MACC details, or managing credits\\n- **No sample test files exist**: The `tests/` directory has no `Samples/` subdirectory and no `Sample*.cs` files, so no test-backed snippets are available to populate the README\\n- **New resource types (MACC, Credits, FreeServices, Discounts, etc.) are completely undocumented**: None of the 8 new resource types added by the migration are mentioned in the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File 1: `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/CHANGELOG.md`** — Edit\\n\\nUpdate the `## 1.0.0-beta.5 (Unreleased)` section to document the TypeSpec migration and new resource types:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.0.0-beta.5 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Migrated package generation from Swagger/AutoRest to TypeSpec.\\n- Added support for `MaccResource` to manage Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment (MACC) resources.\\n- Added support for `CreditResource` and `CreditSourceResource` to manage billing credits and credit sources.\\n- Added support for `FreeServicesResource` to list and manage free service entitlements.\\n- Added support for `ConditionalCreditResource` and `ConditionalCreditContributorResource` to manage conditional credits and their contributors.\\n- Added support for `ContributorResource` to manage billing benefit contributors.\\n- Added support for `DiscountResource` to manage billing discounts.\\n```\\n\\n**File 2: `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/README.md`** — Edit\\n\\nReplace the generic Key Concepts and Examples sections with content specific to this package:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n### BillingBenefits resources\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits` provides management operations for the following resource types:\\n\\n| Resource | Description |\\n|----------|-------------|\\n| `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource` | Represents a savings plan order that groups one or more savings plans. |\\n| `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanResource` | Represents an individual savings plan within a savings plan order. |\\n| `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderAliasResource` | An alias used to create or reference a savings plan order. |\\n| `BillingBenefitsReservationOrderAliasResource` | An alias used to create or reference a reservation order. |\\n| `MaccResource` | Represents a Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment (MACC). |\\n| `CreditResource` | Represents a billing credit associated with a billing account. |\\n| `CreditSourceResource` | Represents the source of a billing credit. |\\n| `FreeServicesResource` | Represents free service entitlements for a billing account. |\\n| `ConditionalCreditResource` | Represents a conditional credit for a billing account. |\\n| `ConditionalCreditContributorResource` | Represents a contributor to a conditional credit. |\\n| `ContributorResource` | Represents a billing benefits contributor. |\\n| `DiscountResource` | Represents a discount associated with a billing account. |\\n\\nFor more information about Azure savings plans, see [Azure savings plans overview]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new sample test file at `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/tests/Samples/Sample1_SavingsPlans.cs`:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_SavingsPlans\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task GetSavingsPlanOrders()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:BillingBenefitsGetSavingsPlanOrders\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            var subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n            // List all savings plan orders accessible to the subscription\\n            await foreach (BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource order in subscription.GetBillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrdersAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Savings Plan Order: {order.Data.Name}, State: {order.Data.ProvisioningState}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task GetSavingsPlan()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:BillingBenefitsGetSavingsPlan\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            var subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n            string savingsPlanOrderId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string savingsPlanId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            // Get a specific savings plan order\\n            BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource order = await subscription\\n                .GetBillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderAsync(savingsPlanOrderId);\\n\\n            // Get a specific savings plan within the order\\n            BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanResource savingsPlan = await order\\n                .GetBillingBenefitsSavingsPlanAsync(savingsPlanId);\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Savings Plan: {savingsPlan.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Term: {savingsPlan.Data.Properties?.Term}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nAdd snippet placeholders to the README `Examples` This migration added **8 new resource types** to the package that were not present before:\\n\\n- `MaccResource` / `MaccData` / `MaccCollection` (Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment)\\n- `CreditResource` / `CreditData` / `CreditCollection`\\n- `CreditSourceResource` / `CreditSourceData` / `CreditSourceCollection`\\n- `FreeServicesResource` / `FreeServicesData` / `FreeServicesCollection`\\n- `ConditionalCreditResource` / `ConditionalCreditData`\\n- `ConditionalCreditContributorResource` / `ConditionalCreditContributorData`\\n- `ContributorResource`\\n- `DiscountResource` / `DiscountCollection`\\n\\nThe pre-existing resources (`BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource`, `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanResource`, `BillingBenefitsReservationOrderAliasResource`, `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderAliasResource`) remain.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG empty for v1.0.0-beta.5**: The unreleased changelog entry has no content despite the significant TypeSpec migration adding 8+ new resource types\\n- **README Key Concepts section is generic**: Only links to the Azure SDK intro page; does not describe any of the actual BillingBenefits resources (SavingsPlans, Reservations, MACC, Credits, FreeServices, ConditionalCredits, Discounts, Contributors) or what each is used for\\n- **README Examples section lacks code snippets**: Only links to a generic samples page; no inline code examples demonstrating common operations like listing savings plans, getting MACC details, or managing credits\\n- **No sample test files exist**: The `tests/` directory has no `Samples/` subdirectory and no `Sample*.cs` files, so no test-backed snippets are available to populate the README\\n- **New resource types (MACC, Credits, FreeServices, Discounts, etc.) are completely undocumented**: None of the 8 new resource types added by the migration are mentioned in the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File 1: `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/CHANGELOG.md`** — Edit\\n\\nUpdate the `## 1.0.0-beta.5 (Unreleased)` section to document the TypeSpec migration and new resource types:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.0.0-beta.5 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Migrated package generation from Swagger/AutoRest to TypeSpec.\\n- Added support for `MaccResource` to manage Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment (MACC) resources.\\n- Added support for `CreditResource` and `CreditSourceResource` to manage billing credits and credit sources.\\n- Added support for `FreeServicesResource` to list and manage free service entitlements.\\n- Added support for `ConditionalCreditResource` and `ConditionalCreditContributorResource` to manage conditional credits and their contributors.\\n- Added support for `ContributorResource` to manage billing benefit contributors.\\n- Added support for `DiscountResource` to manage billing discounts.\\n```\\n\\n**File 2: `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/README.md`** — Edit\\n\\nReplace the generic Key Concepts and Examples sections with content specific to this package:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n### BillingBenefits resources\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits` provides management operations for the following resource types:\\n\\n| Resource | Description |\\n|----------|-------------|\\n| `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource` | Represents a savings plan order that groups one or more savings plans. |\\n| `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanResource` | Represents an individual savings plan within a savings plan order. |\\n| `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderAliasResource` | An alias used to create or reference a savings plan order. |\\n| `BillingBenefitsReservationOrderAliasResource` | An alias used to create or reference a reservation order. |\\n| `MaccResource` | Represents a Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment (MACC). |\\n| `CreditResource` | Represents a billing credit associated with a billing account. |\\n| `CreditSourceResource` | Represents the source of a billing credit. |\\n| `FreeServicesResource` | Represents free service entitlements for a billing account. |\\n| `ConditionalCreditResource` | Represents a conditional credit for a billing account. |\\n| `ConditionalCreditContributorResource` | Represents a contributor to a conditional credit. |\\n| `ContributorResource` | Represents a billing benefits contributor. |\\n| `DiscountResource` | Represents a discount associated with a billing account. |\\n\\nFor more information about Azure savings plans, see [Azure savings plans overview]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new sample .GetBillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderAsync(savingsPlanOrderId);\\n\\n            // Get a specific savings plan within the order\\n            BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanResource savingsPlan = await This migration added **8 new resource types** to the package that were not present before:\\n\\n- `MaccResource` / `MaccData` / `MaccCollection` (Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment)\\n- `CreditResource` / `CreditData` / `CreditCollection`\\n- `CreditSourceResource` / `CreditSourceData` / `CreditSourceCollection`\\n- `FreeServicesResource` / `FreeServicesData` / `FreeServicesCollection`\\n- `ConditionalCreditResource` / `ConditionalCreditData`\\n- `ConditionalCreditContributorResource` / `ConditionalCreditContributorData`\\n- `ContributorResource`\\n- `DiscountResource` / `DiscountCollection`\\n\\nThe pre-existing resources (`BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource`, `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanResource`, `BillingBenefitsReservationOrderAliasResource`, `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderAliasResource`) remain.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG empty for v1.0.0-beta.5**: The unreleased changelog entry has no content despite the significant TypeSpec migration adding 8+ new resource types\\n- **README Key Concepts section is generic**: Only links to the Azure SDK intro page; does not describe any of the actual BillingBenefits resources (SavingsPlans, Reservations, MACC, Credits, FreeServices, ConditionalCredits, Discounts, Contributors) or what each is used for\\n- **README Examples section lacks code snippets**: Only links to a generic samples page; no inline code examples demonstrating common operations like listing savings plans, getting MACC details, or managing credits\\n- **No sample test files exist**: The `tests/` directory has no `Samples/` subdirectory and no `Sample*.cs` files, so no test-backed snippets are available to populate the README\\n- **New resource types (MACC, Credits, FreeServices, Discounts, etc.) are completely undocumented**: None of the 8 new resource types added by the migration are mentioned in the README\\n\\n\\u003cdetails\\u003e\\n\\u003csummary\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\u003c/summary\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File 1: `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/CHANGELOG.md`** — Edit\\n\\nUpdate the `## 1.0.0-beta.5 (Unreleased)` section to document the TypeSpec migration and new resource types:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.0.0-beta.5 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Migrated package generation from Swagger/AutoRest to TypeSpec.\\n- Added support for `MaccResource` to manage Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment (MACC) resources.\\n- Added support for `CreditResource` and `CreditSourceResource` to manage billing credits and credit sources.\\n- Added support for `FreeServicesResource` to list and manage free service entitlements.\\n- Added support for `ConditionalCreditResource` and `ConditionalCreditContributorResource` to manage conditional credits and their contributors.\\n- Added support for `ContributorResource` to manage billing benefit contributors.\\n- Added support for `DiscountResource` to manage billing discounts.\\n```\\n\\n**File 2: `sdk/billingbenefits/Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits/README.md`** — Edit\\n\\nReplace the generic Key Concepts and Examples sections with content specific to this package:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n### BillingBenefits resources\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.BillingBenefits` provides management operations for the following resource types:\\n\\n| Resource | Description |\\n|----------|-------------|\\n| `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderResource` | Represents a savings plan order that groups one or more savings plans. |\\n| `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanResource` | Represents an individual savings plan within a savings plan order. |\\n| `BillingBenefitsSavingsPlanOrderAliasResource` | An alias used to create or reference a savings plan order. |\\n| `BillingBenefitsReservationOrderAliasResource` | An alias used to create or reference a reservation order. |\\n| `MaccResource` | Represents a Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment (MACC). |\\n| `CreditResource` | Represents a billing credit associated with a billing account. |\\n| `CreditSourceResource` | Represents the source of a billing credit. |\\n| `FreeServicesResource` | Represents free service entitlements for a billing account. |\\n| `ConditionalCreditResource` | Represents a conditional credit for a billing account. |\\n| `ConditionalCreditContributorResource` | Represents a contributor to a conditional credit. |\\n| `ContributorResource` | Represents a billing benefits contributor. |\\n| `DiscountResource` | Represents a discount associated with a billing account. |\\n\\nFor more information about Azure savings plans, see [Azure savings plans overview]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new sample test file at
